title: Within-Lap Pairwise Ranking Sidecar
summary: ROC AUC is a ranking metric, and CatBoost supports pairwise/groupwise ranking objectives when GroupId is provided.
rationale: ROC AUC is a ranking metric, and CatBoost supports pairwise/groupwise ranking objectives when GroupId is provided. In this task, many hard decisions are relative within the same race-lap field state, so a sidecar trained to rank which cars are most pit-likely within a (Year,Race,LapNumber) group could add useful diversity versus pure Logloss models.
implementation_hint: Build a second model with GroupId=(Year,Race,LapNumber) and either PairLogit or a ranking objective, generating pairs only within each lap group. Blend its OOF scores with the existing classifier rather than replacing the baseline; keep standard binary models as anchors.
expected_effect: Can sharpen ordering among same-lap candidates and improve AUC even if absolute probabilities remain imperfect.
risk: This reframes the task and may underperform on laps with very few positives; keep it as an ensemble member, not the only model.
